description: Replace broken resolve_client + 4-Mutex InferenceState with single-owner supervisor task using actor/handle pattern. Eliminates deadlock on engine death, fixes double-spawn at startup, adds auto-restart with backoff.

userStories.title: Split connect_or_spawn into composable primitives
userStories.description: As the supervisor, I need composable spawn/health/policy primitives so I can orchestrate the engine lifecycle with fine-grained control instead of calling the monolithic connect_or_spawn.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract spawn_engine(options) -> Result<u32> from spawn_host — spawns detached process, returns PID, does NOT wait for health
Extract wait_for_healthy(client, timeout) -> Result<()> from the health poll loop in connect_or_spawn
Extract check_running_policy(client, force_override, force_respawn) -> Result<RunningHostPolicyDecision> from enforce_running_host_policy
Extract shutdown_and_wait(client, timeout) -> Result<()> from request_engine_shutdown + wait_for_engine_down
Extract kill_stale_processes() from kill_stale_engine_processes (make pub)
Extract with_spawn_lock(dir, f) -> Result<T> wrapper around acquire_spawn_lock
connect_or_spawn still exists as convenience wrapper calling these primitives — no behavior change
cargo test -p smolpc-engine-client passes
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: File: engine/crates/smolpc-engine-client/src/spawn.rs. Keep all existing tests passing. The primitives must be pub so the supervisor can call them directly.

userStories.title: Create EngineLifecycleState enum and EngineCommand types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the state machine types so the supervisor can model lifecycle transitions explicitly.
acceptanceCriteria: EngineLifecycleState enum with variants: Idle, Starting, WaitingForHealth, Running { backend: Option<String>, model_id: Option<String> }, Crashed { message: String, restart_count: u32 }, Failed { message: String }
Derive serde::Serialize with #[serde(tag = "state", rename_all = "snake_case")] for Tauri event emission
Derive Debug, Clone, PartialEq
Add is_running(), is_terminal(), can_transition_to(&self, next) -> bool methods
EngineCommand enum: Start { config, respond_to }, SetRuntimeMode { mode, respond_to }, SetDesiredModel { model_id }, RefreshStatus, Shutdown { respond_to }
StartupConfig struct: runtime_mode, dml_device_id, default_model_id, startup_mode
New file: apps/codehelper/src-tauri/src/engine/mod.rs (add mod engine to lib.rs)
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: Use tokio::sync::oneshot::Sender for respond_to fields. Import RuntimeModePreference and StartupMode from smolpc_engine_client.

userStories.title: Build EngineSupervisorHandle with get_client and state access
userStories.description: As a Tauri command handler, I need a Clone-able handle to communicate with the supervisor without any Mutex access.
acceptanceCriteria: EngineSupervisorHandle struct with mpsc::Sender<EngineCommand> and watch::Receiver<EngineLifecycleState>
current_state() -> EngineLifecycleState — reads watch channel borrow, instant, no lock
get_client(timeout) -> Result<EngineClient, String> — waits on watch::Receiver::changed() until Running state, returns clone of cached client
get_client_if_ready() -> Option<EngineClient> — non-blocking, returns cached client only if Running
ensure_started(config) -> Result<(), String> — sends Start command via mpsc, receives response via oneshot
set_runtime_mode(mode) -> Result<(), String> — sends SetRuntimeMode command, receives response via oneshot
shutdown() -> Result<(), String> — sends Shutdown command, receives response via oneshot
refresh_status() — sends RefreshStatus command (fire-and-forget, no response needed)
Handle is Clone (mpsc::Sender and watch::Receiver are both Clone)
New file: apps/codehelper/src-tauri/src/engine/handle.rs
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: EngineClient is #[derive(Clone)] with Arc-based reqwest::Client — cloning is cheap. The handle stores a cached_client: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Option<EngineClient>>> updated by a background watcher when Running state is broadcast.

userStories.title: Build EngineSupervisor core loop with health monitoring
userStories.description: As the app, I need the supervisor background task that owns the engine lifecycle — the core select! loop with health checking and command handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Supervisor struct owns: client (Option<EngineClient>), runtime_config, desired_model, engine_pid (Option<u32>), restart_count, last_restart_window_start
Core loop uses tokio::select! with three arms: cmd_rx.recv(), health_interval.tick() (10s), conditional sleep(restart_delay)
Health check arm: if Running state, check PID alive (OS-level) + client.health() HTTP call
On health failure: transition to Crashed, broadcast state, schedule restart
Command handling: Start, SetRuntimeMode, SetDesiredModel, RefreshStatus, Shutdown
Broadcasts state via watch::Sender after every transition
Emits Tauri events via app_handle.emit("engine-state-changed", &state)
New file: apps/codehelper/src-tauri/src/engine/supervisor.rs
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: Use the Phase 0 primitives (spawn_engine, wait_for_healthy, etc.) from smolpc-engine-client. The supervisor task is spawned but NOT yet wired into Tauri managed state — that comes in US-006.

userStories.title: Add spawn and restart logic to supervisor
userStories.description: As the supervisor, I need to spawn the engine process, handle restarts with exponential backoff, and regenerate tokens on death.
acceptanceCriteria: do_spawn_sequence(): resolves binary path, builds EngineConnectOptions, calls spawn_engine + wait_for_healthy using Phase 0 primitives
On Crashed -> Starting: delete old token file, regenerate via load_or_create_token, construct new EngineClient with fresh token, kill stale processes, spawn
Exponential backoff: restart delays of 1s, 2s, 4s for consecutive crashes
Max 3 restarts within 5 minutes — after limit, transition to Failed
On reaching Running: if desired_model is set, call client.load_model() to restore it
On RefreshStatus command: call client.status() and update Running state fields (backend, model_id)
Binary resolution uses same fallback chain as current resolve_host_binary_path() — works in dev and production
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: Token path: shared_runtime_dir/engine-token.txt. PID file: shared_runtime_dir/engine.pid. Spawn log: shared_runtime_dir/engine-spawn.log. Use DETACHED_PROCESS | CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP on Windows.

userStories.title: Wire supervisor into Tauri Builder::setup and manage state
userStories.description: As the app, I need the supervisor to start on app launch and be available as Tauri managed state alongside the existing InferenceState.
acceptanceCriteria: In Builder::setup(): create mpsc channel (buffer=16), create watch channel (initial=Idle), spawn supervisor task, app.manage(EngineSupervisorHandle)
Supervisor receives app_handle clone for event emission and path resolution
Both InferenceState AND EngineSupervisorHandle are registered as managed state (coexist during migration)
On RunEvent::ExitRequested: send Shutdown command to supervisor (via handle or by dropping cmd_tx)
App starts, supervisor logs lifecycle transitions, engine spawns via supervisor
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: File: apps/codehelper/src-tauri/src/lib.rs. The supervisor runs ALONGSIDE existing InferenceState during migration phases. Both are available to Tauri commands.

userStories.title: Migrate state-mutating Tauri commands to supervisor (Group A)
userStories.description: As the app, I need state-mutating commands to go through the supervisor first so the old resolve_client path can't kill supervisor-managed engines.
acceptanceCriteria: engine_ensure_started: use supervisor.ensure_started(config).await instead of apply_runtime_mode_preference + resolve_client
set_inference_runtime_mode: use supervisor.set_runtime_mode(mode).await instead of resolve_client(true) + rollback
load_model: use supervisor.get_client(60s).await then client.load_model(), call supervisor.refresh_status() after
unload_model: use supervisor.get_client(60s).await then client.unload_model(), call supervisor.refresh_status() after
All four commands take State<EngineSupervisorHandle> instead of State<InferenceState>
cargo check --workspace clean
cargo clippy --workspace clean
userStories.notes: These MUST migrate before read-only commands to prevent the old path from killing supervisor-managed engines. File: apps/codehelper/src-tauri/src/commands/engine_client_adapter.rs and inference.rs.

userStories.title: Migrate read-only and generation Tauri commands to supervisor (Groups B+C)
userStories.description: As the app, I need all remaining engine-touching commands to use the supervisor handle.
acceptanceCriteria: list_models, get_current_model, get_inference_backend_status, engine_status: use supervisor.get_client(60s).await
check_model_readiness, check_model_exists, is_generating: use supervisor.get_client(60s).await
inference_generate, inference_generate_messages, inference_cancel: use supervisor.get_client(60s).await
assistant_send (3 match arms): use supervisor.get_client(60s).await
assistant_cancel: use supervisor.get_client_if_ready() (non-blocking)
All commands take State<EngineSupervisorHandle>
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: Files: inference.rs, engine_client_adapter.rs, assistant.rs. Remove resolve_generation_client and cached_generation_client usages.

userStories.title: Migrate special-case commands and delete old InferenceState
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the health/memory commands migrated and all old Mutex-based code removed.
acceptanceCriteria: engine_health_only: use supervisor.current_state().is_running() — instant, no HTTP
evaluate_memory_pressure: use supervisor.get_client_if_ready() — non-blocking
Delete InferenceState struct and all methods (check_health, cached_healthy_client, shutdown_engine)
Delete resolve_client, resolve_generation_client, cached_generation_client functions
Delete ensure_desired_model_loaded, apply_runtime_mode_preference functions
Clean up all Arc<Mutex<>> imports from inference.rs
cargo check --workspace clean
cargo clippy --workspace clean — no dead code warnings
userStories.notes: This is the point of no return — after this, only the supervisor pattern exists. Verify thoroughly.

userStories.title: Add EngineLifecycleState TypeScript type and store update method
userStories.description: As the frontend, I need the TypeScript type for lifecycle events and a store method to map them to existing reactive state.
acceptanceCriteria: TypeScript type EngineLifecycleState defined in src/lib/types/inference.ts with all 6 variants (idle, starting, waiting_for_health, running, crashed, failed)
inferenceStore.updateLifecycleState(state) method added to inference.svelte.ts
Method maps: running(model_id=some) -> engineHealthy=true, isReady=true; crashed/failed -> engineHealthy=false, error=message; starting -> engineHealthy=false
forceResetGenerationState() called when transitioning to crashed or failed
svelte-check 0 errors
userStories.notes: Keep existing EngineReadinessDto — it's still used by engine_status and engine_ensure_started return values. The lifecycle state is a higher-level abstraction.

userStories.title: Replace frontend health polling with Tauri event listener
userStories.description: As a student, I want the UI to reactively reflect engine state changes without polling delays.
acceptanceCriteria: Health polling $effect (setTimeout chain) deleted from App.svelte
reestablishEngineSession() function deleted
reconnectingEngineSession state variable deleted
checkHealth() invoke removed from health polling (supervisor handles it)
Tauri event listener added in onMount: listen('engine-state-changed', handler) calling inferenceStore.updateLifecycleState()
unlisten called in cleanup/onDestroy
Banner condition updated: show when lifecycleState is crashed or failed
StartupLoadingScreen shows for starting, waiting_for_health, running(model_id=null)
svelte-check 0 errors
eslint 0 errors on changed files
userStories.notes: Use @tauri-apps/api/event listen() function. Import EngineLifecycleState type. The initInference() function should still call ensureStarted() — it now just waits on the supervisor internally.

userStories.title: Migrate launcher to shared supervisor handle
userStories.description: As the launcher, I need to share the same engine supervisor instead of maintaining a duplicate client cache.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_engine_client() in orchestrator.rs replaced with supervisor.get_client(60s).await
Launcher's separate EngineClient cache deleted
Launcher's separate connect lock and runtime config deleted
LauncherState takes State<EngineSupervisorHandle> instead of managing its own client
cargo check --workspace clean
userStories.notes: File: apps/codehelper/src-tauri/src/launcher/orchestrator.rs. Simple replacement — launcher is a read-only consumer.
